channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Man accused of fencing stolen items linked to theft at star athlete's Ohio home, prosecutors say
item.description: One of two men accused in New York City of fencing luxury goods stolen from homes across the country has been linked to a theft at the residence of a “high-profile athlete in Ohio.”.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he wants US to take charge of Gaza Strip at press conference with Netanyahu
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip and redevelop it after Palestinians are resettled elsewhere. He made the remarks in a joint press conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Residents call out Chicago officials for 'harmful' display depicting Uncle Sam, Netanyahu as murderers
item.description: Chicago residents have been divided by a controversial art display depicting Benjamin Netanyahu and Uncle Sam as murderers, with some accusing it of being antisemitic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Russian, Uzbek nationals filed 132 fake voter applications in Pinellas County: DOJ
item.description: The 132 applications were submitted in envelopes with "identically formatted return and address labels," the complaint said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Still locked out of federal funding, several Head Start preschools may need to close temporarily
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Head Start preschools serving thousands of children around the country could be at risk of closing because they remain locked out of their federal funding, a problem that first surfaced last week during President Donald Trump's aborted effort to freeze federal grants.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims of the midair collision near DC recovered as NTSB probes altitude data
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — The remains of all 67 victims of last week's midair collision of an American Airlines flight and an Army helicopter near the nation's capital have been recovered, authorities said Tuesday. All but one has been identified.
item.dc:creator: SERKAN GURBUZ and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of artists contribute songs for L.A. fire benefit album available for 1 day only
item.description: Dozens of renowned indie artists are coming together to help the Los Angeles area recover from deadly wildfires by releasing a massive compilation album available for one day only. The new 90-track compilation album features artists such as R.E.M.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Former councilman's wife alleges revenge porn, charge each other with assault
item.description: Documents show that Perry filed charges against Barnes of dissemination or sale of the image of another.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, NewsNation

item.title: Treasury tells Congress that DOGE has ‘Read Only’ access to payment systems
item.description: A Treasury Department official has written a letter to federal lawmakers saying that a tech executive working with Elon Musk’s “Department of Government Efficiency” will have “read-only access” to the government’s payment system.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak & Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma Gov. Stitt announces DOGE-OK
item.description: According to the Governor's Office, DOGE-OK will build upon past efficiency initiatives and focus on eliminating wasteful government spending.
item.dc:creator: Terré Gables/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender athletes from women’s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order Wednesday banning transgender athletes from competing in girls’ and women’s sports, the latest in a string of executive actions from the new administration to take aim at LGBTQ rights.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump suggests displaced Palestinians in Gaza be 'permanently' resettled outside war-torn territory
item.description: The renewed call by the Trump administration on Arab nations to relocate displaced Palestinians comes as Trump is set to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the White House later Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about federal tax credits
item.description: Tax credits are often more valuable compared to deductions because they directly reduce your tax bill dollar-for-dollar.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China counters with tariffs on US products. It will also investigate Google
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China countered President Donald Trump's across-the-board tariffs on Chinese products with tariffs on select U.S. imports Tuesday, as well as announcing an antitrust investigation into Google and other trade measures. U.S.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and HUIZHONG WU Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico after they pledge to boost border enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday agreed to a 30-day pause on his tariff threats against Mexico and Canada as America's two largest trading partners took steps to appease his concerns about border security and drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary deportation protection for 350,000 Venezuelans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is ending protections that shielded roughly 350,000 Venezuelans from deportation, leaving them with two months before they lose their right to work in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, WGN 9

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KTLA 5

item.title: LA wildfires: Famed Pacific Coast Highway reopens nearly 1 month after disaster
item.description: The Pacific Coast Highway in Malibu, California, partially reopened on Monday, for the first time since fires tore through neighborhoods, destroying homes and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas National Guard can now arrest and detain people who illegally enter the US
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas National Guard soldiers can arrest and detain people for entering the U.S. illegally from Mexico under an agreement with the Trump administration that expands the military's role in immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Salvage crews recover engine, large portion of jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have recovered an engine and large pieces of fuselage and are working to retrieve a wing from the wreckage of a commercial airliner involved in last week's midair collision near Washington’s Reagan National Airport, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starbucks introduces Valentine's Day drinks, new refreshers
item.description: Instead of stuffed teddy bears or roses, Starbucks is gifting its customers new menu offerings this Valentine’s Day. On Monday, the coffee chain announced that it will be adding Blackberry Sage Refreshers to menus for a limited time.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: MLB ump fired amid gambling probe
item.description: Major League Baseball has fired an umpire as part of an investigation into sports gambling. Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball, according to ESPN reporter Jesse Rogers and later confirmed by the league.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Multiple Canadian provinces pull US-made booze over Trump tariffs
item.description: Nova Scotia Premier Tim Houston said the Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation will remove all U.S. alcohol from store shelves starting Tuesday, totaling more than 400 products, including beer, wine, spirits and coolers.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Here's the breakdown
item.description: Experts say every state gets millions in federal help — and a little more goes to Republican states and congressional districts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Families visit DC crash site days after the deadliest US air disaster in a generation
item.description: Dozens of people walked along the banks of the Potomac River near Reagan National Airport, close to where an American Airlines jet and an Army Black Hawk helicopter collided Wednesday, killing all 67 aboard.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to save money on your taxes
item.description: You may qualify for tax credits for caring for a child or dependent or investing in clean energy for your home.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Opinion: Why Trump's meme coin is a cash grab
item.description: The coin – officially called $Trump – briefly ascended into the top 15 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization and attracted over a half-million buyers.
item.dc:creator: Maximilian Brichta Doctoral Student of Communication, University of Southern California, ABC 4
